title: Smoked Haddock Harees
description: A traditional Middle Eastern dish made with smoked haddock and harees, perfect for a comforting breakfast
instructions: Rinse the smoked haddock fillets under cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
In a large pot, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it's translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for another minute, stirring constantly.
Add the harees and 4 cups of water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the smoked haddock fillets to the pot and cook for another 5-7 minutes, or until they're cooked through.
Stir in the chopped cilantro, salt, and black pepper.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the top and serve hot.
